- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- optimize: Fully autonomous RVV optimization loop for libnn functions. Target VPU, toolchain, build flags, FPGA board, and hardware profile all come from config.toml — no hardcoded paths or CPU names. Runs a 7-step FSM — validate, select, analyze, implement, verify, measure, learn — then loops back to analyze with new insights. No human intervention required.
- hot-inner-kernel-extractor: Extract a libnn function's hot inner-most loop into a standalone, mcycle-wrapped RISC-V assembly harness so it can be measured on a cycle-accurate Verilator simulator (or any cycle counter) without running the whole function. Use this skill whenever the user wants to study pipeline behavior of a libnn kernel, profile inner-loop cycles, build a microbenchmark from an existing function, prepare a Verilator workload, isolate a hot loop body, or asks about "extracting", "isolating", "harnessing", "microbenchmark", "Verilator timeview", "pipeline timeview", "steady state", or "inner kernel" of any libnn / RVV function. The skill identifies the hottest translation block via QEMU's hotblocks plugin, walks objdump to find the innermost backward branch, computes live-in registers, and emits a compilable .S harness that runs the body N times under csrr mcycle.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Skill `hot-inner-kernel-extractor` is built and exercised on the example.

## Summary

**Skill location:** `.claude/skills/hot-inner-kernel-extractor/`

**Pipeline (~3-min end-to-end):**
1. `run_hotblocks.sh` — compiles unit test, runs QEMU + libhotblocks plugin
2. `find_hot_loop.py` — call-graph filter, picks hottest in-scope block, finds innermost backward branch, computes live-ins via read-before-write analysis (handles `nds.vd4dots` RMW)
3. `emit_harness.py` — heuristic live-in init, warmup + `mcycle`-wrapped measure loop, rewrites mid-loop branches (in-body→local labels, out-of-body→`_oob_exit` poison tripwire)
4. `write_report.py` — analysis.md

**Result on `riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any`:**
- Hot kernel correctly identified as `nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_unroll4` (the 4×4 vd4dots GEMM unroll)
- Loop body: 72 instructions, software-pipelined (v16..v23 / v24..v31 alternate as load/compute pairs)
- Live-ins: 12 scalar + 24 vector — correctly captures the SW-pipeline state
- `harness.s` assembles cleanly with libnn's toolchain (`-c` produces 200KB object)

**Files:** `work/<fn>/{analysis.json, analysis.md, harness.s}`. Evals at `evals/evals.json` for future iteration. Next step on your end: link `harness.s` against your Verilator testbench's linker script and read `cycle_total` symbol from sim memory.

`/home/nick/work/libnn_0421/.claude/skills/hot-inner-kernel-extractor/work/riscv_nn_conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any/harness.s`

Sibling artifacts in the same directory:
- `analysis.json` - machine-readable result
- `analysis.md` - human-readable report
- `harness.o` - assembled object (from my smoke test)

message.content: could you run the harness.s on FPGA?

content.text:
Honest answer: not without modification. The harness is bare-metal `_start` with an infinite spin at `_exit` — it has no print, no exit, no startup compatible with the AE350 board's boot flow. The existing FPGA path (`run_fpga_test.sh`) loads ELFs that link with `ae350-xip_hvm.ld`, use crt0, call `printf` over semihosting, and exit cleanly.

Let me check what's actually needed and try.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	################################################################################
3	# Build and run a single libnn unit test on the FPGA board via GDB.
4	# Captures ground-truth cycle and instruction counts from PF_COUNTER.
5	#
6	# All target-specific values (toolchain, host:port, linker script, sim
7	# wrapper, CFLAGS) come from config.toml.
8	#
9	# Usage:
10	#   run_fpga_test.sh <test_name> [--config <config.toml>] [--timeout N]
11	#
12	# Output:
13	#   ${CFG_WORK_DIR}/t_<test>_perf.adx         - linked ELF
14	#   ${CFG_WORK_DIR}/t_<test>_perf.objdump     - disassembly
15	#   ${CFG_WORK_DIR}/fpga_<test>.log           - raw GDB log
16	################################################################################
17	set -uo pipefail
18	
19	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
20	
21	TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE=""
22	TEST_NAME=""
23	CONFIG_TOML=""
24	
25	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
26	    case "$1" in
27	        --timeout) TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
28	        --config)  CONFIG_TOML="$2"; shift 2 ;;
29	        -*)        echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
30	        *)         TEST_NAME="$1"; shift ;;
31	    esac
32	done
33	
34	if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
35	    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--config <config.toml>] [--timeout N]" >&2
36	    exit 1
37	fi
38	
39	# shellcheck disable=SC1091
40	source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/parse_config.sh" ${CONFIG_TOML:+"${CONFIG_TOML}"}
41	
42	TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE:-${CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT}}"
43	UNIT_TEST_DIR="${CFG_LIBNN_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
44	TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
45	WORK_DIR="${CFG_WORK_DIR}"
46	LIBNN="${CFG_LIBNN_ROOT}/libnn.a"
47	ADX="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}_perf.adx"
48	OBJDUMP_FILE="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}_perf.objdump"
49	LOG="${WORK_DIR}/fpga_${TEST_NAME}.log"
50	
51	# FPGA board connection (from config.toml)
52	export MYPC="${CFG_FPGA_HOST}"
53	export MYPORT="${CFG_FPGA_PORT}"
54	
55	for f in "${CFG_CC}" "${TEST_SRC}" "${CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER}" "${LIBNN}" "${CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT}"; do
56	    [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "ERROR: Not found: $f" >&2; exit 1; }
57	done
58	
59	# The GDB expect wrapper hardcodes a "gdb-nopython" binary name. If the
60	# toolchain has gdb but not gdb-nopython, expose a symlinked alias.
61	GDB_NOPY="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN}/${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gdb-nopython"
62	GDB_EXTRA_PATH=""
63	if [ -f "${CFG_GDB}" ] && [ ! -e "${GDB_NOPY}" ]; then
64	    GDB_TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d /tmp/opt_fpga_gdb_XXXXXX)
65	    ln -sf "${CFG_GDB}" "${GDB_TMPDIR}/${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gdb-nopython"
66	    GDB_EXTRA_PATH="${GDB_TMPDIR}:"
67	fi
68	
69	export PATH="${GDB_EXTRA_PATH}${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN}:${CFG_LIBNN_ROOT}:${PATH}"
70	mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"
71	
72	# Compile: base + perf-specific flags. The -DGETENV_TESTDIR + linker flags
73	# are driven by WORK semantics, not by target, so kept as local.
74	INC="-I${CFG_LIBNN_ROOT}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include -I${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN}/../riscv64-elf/include"
75	CFLAGS_LINK="-Wl,-gc-sections"
76	CFLAGS_DATA="-DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"
77	CFLAGS_ALL="${CFG_CFLAGS_BASE} ${CFG_CFLAGS_PERF} ${CFLAGS_LINK} ${CFLAGS_DATA}"
78	
79	echo "[compile] ${TEST_NAME} (perf build)"
80	${CFG_CC} ${CFLAGS_ALL} ${INC} -o "${ADX}" "${TEST_SRC}" "${LIBNN}" -lm 2>&1
81	if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
82	    echo "RESULT: COMPILE_ERROR" >&2
83	    exit 1
84	fi
85	
86	${CFG_OBJDUMP} -d "${ADX}" > "${OBJDUMP_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
87	
88	# Reset board state
89	[ -z "${CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG}" ] || rm -f "${CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG}"
90	
91	echo "[run] FPGA board ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (timeout=${TIMEOUT}s)"
92	timeout "${TIMEOUT}" "${CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER}" "${ADX}" 2>&1 | tee "${LOG}"
93	FPGA_RC=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
94	
95	echo
96	if [ "${FPGA_RC}" -eq 124 ]; then
97	    echo "RESULT: TIMEOUT (${TIMEOUT}s)"
98	    exit 1
99	fi
100	
101	CYCLE_COUNT=$(grep -o "The cycle count is [0-9]*" "${LOG}" | tail -1 | grep -o "[0-9]*" || echo "")
102	INST_COUNT=$( grep -o "The inst count is [0-9]*"  "${LOG}" | tail -1 | grep -o "[0-9]*" || echo "")
103	
104	if grep -qi "accuracy checking pass" "${LOG}"; then
105	    PASS_COUNT=$(grep -ci "accuracy checking pass" "${LOG}" || true)
106	    FAIL_COUNT=$(grep -ci "accuracy checking fail" "${LOG}" || true)
107	
108	    echo "--- FPGA Results ---"
109	    echo "  Cycles:       ${CYCLE_COUNT:-unknown}"
110	    echo "  Instructions: ${INST_COUNT:-unknown}"
111	    if [ -n "${CYCLE_COUNT}" ] && [ -n "${INST_COUNT}" ]; then
112	        IPC=$(python3 -c "print(f'{${INST_COUNT}/${CYCLE_COUNT}:.2f}')" 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
113	        echo "  IPC:          ${IPC}"
114	    fi
115	    echo "  Accuracy:     ${PASS_COUNT} pass, ${FAIL_COUNT} fail"
116	    echo "  ADX:          ${ADX}"
117	    echo "  Objdump:      ${OBJDUMP_FILE}"
118	    echo "---"
119	
120	    if [ "${FAIL_COUNT}" -gt 0 ]; then
121	        echo "RESULT: FAIL"
122	        exit 1
123	    fi
124	    echo "RESULT: PASS (cycles=${CYCLE_COUNT})"
125	    exit 0
126	else
127	    echo "RESULT: UNKNOWN (no pass/fail detected)"
128	    exit 1
129	fi
130

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Build and run a single libnn unit test on the FPGA board via GDB.
# Captures ground-truth cycle and instruction counts from PF_COUNTER.
#
# All target-specific values (toolchain, host:port, linker script, sim
# wrapper, CFLAGS) come from config.toml.
#
# Usage:
#   run_fpga_test.sh <test_name> [--config <config.toml>] [--timeout N]
#
# Output:
#   ${CFG_WORK_DIR}/t_<test>_perf.adx         - linked ELF
#   ${CFG_WORK_DIR}/t_<test>_perf.objdump     - disassembly
#   ${CFG_WORK_DIR}/fpga_<test>.log           - raw GDB log
################################################################################
set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE=""
TEST_NAME=""
CONFIG_TOML=""

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --timeout) TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --config)  CONFIG_TOML="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        -*)        echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
        *)         TEST_NAME="$1"; shift ;;
    esac
done

if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--config <config.toml>] [--timeout N]" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/parse_config.sh" ${CONFIG_TOML:+"${CONFIG_TOML}"}

TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE:-${CFG_FPGA_TIMEOUT}}"
UNIT_TEST_DIR="${CFG_LIBNN_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
WORK_DIR="${CFG_WORK_DIR}"
LIBNN="${CFG_LIBNN_ROOT}/libnn.a"
ADX="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}_perf.adx"
OBJDUMP_FILE="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}_perf.objdump"
LOG="${WORK_DIR}/fpga_${TEST_NAME}.log"

# FPGA board connection (from config.toml)
export MYPC="${CFG_FPGA_HOST}"
export MYPORT="${CFG_FPGA_PORT}"

for f in "${CFG_CC}" "${TEST_SRC}" "${CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER}" "${LIBNN}" "${CFG_FPGA_LD_SCRIPT}"; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "ERROR: Not found: $f" >&2; exit 1; }
done

# The GDB expect wrapper hardcodes a "gdb-nopython" binary name. If the
# toolchain has gdb but not gdb-nopython, expose a symlinked alias.
GDB_NOPY="${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN}/${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gdb-nopython"
GDB_EXTRA_PATH=""
if [ -f "${CFG_GDB}" ] && [ ! -e "${GDB_NOPY}" ]; then
    GDB_TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d /tmp/opt_fpga_gdb_XXXXXX)
    ln -sf "${CFG_GDB}" "${GDB_TMPDIR}/${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gdb-nopython"
    GDB_EXTRA_PATH="${GDB_TMPDIR}:"
fi

export PATH="${GDB_EXTRA_PATH}${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN}:${CFG_LIBNN_ROOT}:${PATH}"
mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"

# Compile: base + perf-specific flags. The -DGETENV_TESTDIR + linker flags
# are driven by WORK semantics, not by target, so kept as local.
INC="-I${CFG_LIBNN_ROOT}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include -I${CFG_TOOLCHAIN_BIN}/../riscv64-elf/include"
CFLAGS_LINK="-Wl,-gc-sections"
CFLAGS_DATA="-DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"
CFLAGS_ALL="${CFG_CFLAGS_BASE} ${CFG_CFLAGS_PERF} ${CFLAGS_LINK} ${CFLAGS_DATA}"

echo "[compile] ${TEST_NAME} (perf build)"
${CFG_CC} ${CFLAGS_ALL} ${INC} -o "${ADX}" "${TEST_SRC}" "${LIBNN}" -lm 2>&1
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "RESULT: COMPILE_ERROR" >&2
    exit 1
fi

${CFG_OBJDUMP} -d "${ADX}" > "${OBJDUMP_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true

# Reset board state
[ -z "${CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG}" ] || rm -f "${CFG_FPGA_RESET_FLAG}"

echo "[run] FPGA board ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (timeout=${TIMEOUT}s)"
timeout "${TIMEOUT}" "${CFG_FPGA_SIM_WRAPPER}" "${ADX}" 2>&1 | tee "${LOG}"
FPGA_RC=${PIPESTATUS[0]}

echo
if [ "${FPGA_RC}" -eq 124 ]; then
    echo "RESULT: TIMEOUT (${TIMEOUT}s)"
    exit 1
fi

CYCLE_COUNT=$(grep -o "The cycle count is [0-9]*" "${LOG}" | tail -1 | grep -o "[0-9]*" || echo "")
INST_COUNT=$( grep -o "The inst count is [0-9]*"  "${LOG}" | tail -1 | grep -o "[0-9]*" || echo "")

if grep -qi "accuracy checking pass" "${LOG}"; then
    PASS_COUNT=$(grep -ci "accuracy checking pass" "${LOG}" || true)
    FAIL_COUNT=$(grep -ci "accuracy checking fail" "${LOG}" || true)

    echo "--- FPGA Results ---"
    echo "  Cycles:       ${CYCLE_COUNT:-unknown}"
    echo "  Instructions: ${INST_COUNT:-unknown}"
    if [ -n "${CYCLE_COUNT}" ] && [ -n "${INST_COUNT}" ]; then
        IPC=$(python3 -c "print(f'{${INST_COUNT}/${CYCLE_COUNT}:.2f}')" 2>/dev/null || echo "?")
        echo "  IPC:          ${IPC}"
    fi
    echo "  Accuracy:     ${PASS_COUNT} pass, ${FAIL_COUNT} fail"
    echo "  ADX:          ${ADX}"
    echo "  Objdump:      ${OBJDUMP_FILE}"
    echo "---"

    if [ "${FAIL_COUNT}" -gt 0 ]; then
        echo "RESULT: FAIL"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "RESULT: PASS (cycles=${CYCLE_COUNT})"
    exit 0
else
    echo "RESULT: UNKNOWN (no pass/fail detected)"
    exit 1
fi
